intro: Regalia ayabacana, also known as Regelia corymbosa or Ayabacana, is a plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Myoporaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. Bioactive constituents of Regalia ayabacana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations.
